description.content: Endpoints for retrieving activity log entries for an organization.
Returns a paginated list of activity log entries for the specified organization. Activity logs record actions taken by users within the organization.

Only return entries that occurred before this time.
Filter entries by a search term.

A token for fetching the next page of results from a previous paginated response.

Endpoints for listing, retrieving, and managing event types that define the kinds of meetings users can schedule.
Returns a paginated list of event types for the specified user or organization. Event types define the kinds of meetings that can be scheduled, including their duration, location, and availability.

query.description: The URI of the user whose event types to list. Required if organization is not specified.
The URI of the organization whose event types to list. Required if user is not specified.
Filter by active status. When true, only active event types are returned.
Sort order for results. Use name:asc or name:desc.
The number of results to return per page.
A token for fetching the next page of results from a previous paginated response.

response.name: Successfully retrieved the list of event types

Endpoints for creating, listing, retrieving, and deleting webhook subscriptions that receive real-time event notifications.
Returns a paginated list of webhook subscriptions for the specified user or organization. Each subscription defines the events to listen for and the callback URL to notify.

query.description: The URI of the user whose webhook subscriptions to list.
(Required) The URI of the organization whose webhook subscriptions to list.
(Required) The scope of the webhook subscription. Use user for subscriptions scoped to a specific user, or organization for organization-wide subscriptions.
The number of results to return per page.
A token for fetching the next page of results from a previous paginated response.

The Calendly Scheduling API (v2) is a RESTful API that allows developers to programmatically manage scheduling workflows. It provides endpoints for managing users, organizations, event types, scheduled events, invitees, routing forms, availability schedules, and webhook subscriptions. The API uses JSON for request and response bodies, standard HTTP methods, and supports authentication via personal access tokens and OAuth 2.1. Developers can use it to create events on behalf of invitees, retrieve scheduling data, and integrate Calendly functionality directly into their applications.
